name: Presa de Puentes Viejas – circular desde Buitrago de Lozoya
region_intro: Mountain biking around Pantano de Puentes Viejas offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama, featuring routes that traverse mountain landscapes and skirt the edges of the reservoir. The region is characterized by lush pine forests, such as the Pinar de Buitrago, and varied tracks including unpaved sections, paved roads, asphalt, and narrower paths. Mountain formations provide attractive views, and the area is part of a system of reservoirs fed by the Lozoya River. This environment provides a range of riding experiences for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Pantano de Puentes Viejas The most popular mountain bike trail is Presa de Puentes Viejas – circular desde Buitrago de Lozoya, an easy 13.3 miles (21.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from Buitrago del Lozoya, a moderate 24.0 miles (38.6 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the Lozoya River and the El Atazar Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint – Matafrailes Bridge loop from Buitrago del Lozoya, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Pantano de Puentes Viejas is defined by reservoir edges, mountain landscapes, and pine forests, with routes combining unpaved tracks and paved sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Pantano de Puentes Viejas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pantano de Puentes Viejas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails available around Pantano de Puentes Viejas, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These include 7 easy routes, 37 moderate routes, and 32 difficult routes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The trails around Pantano de Puentes Viejas offer diverse terrain, including unpaved tracks, paved sections, asphalt, and narrower paths. You'll ride through mountain landscapes, along the reservoir's edges, and through lush pine forests like the Pinar de Buitrago, providing a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962650182/presa-de-puentes-viejas-circular-desde-buitrago-de-lozoya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Puentes Viejas – circular desde Buitrago de Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21.4 km route that offers panoramic views around the reservoir.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 32 difficult routes in the area. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23758337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint – Matafrailes Bridge loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.3 km trail leading through varied and demanding terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: While mountain biking, you can enjoy stunning natural features such as the tranquil waters of the Pantano de Puentes Viejas itself, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2559384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lozoya River&lt;/a&gt;, and the surrounding pine forests like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de las Gariñas&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter other reservoirs like Embalse del Villar and Embalse del Atazar, and unique rocky areas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can visit the medieval town of Buitrago del Lozoya, home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4646044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Mendoza Family&lt;/a&gt; and its historic walls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes Viejas Dam&lt;/a&gt; itself is an engineering marvel, and historical bunkers from the 'Frente del Agua' near Paredes de Buitrago offer a glimpse into the past.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of the reservoirs and surrounding mountains, and the combination of unpaved tracks and paved sections that make for an engaging ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 38.6 km circular path offering scenic views.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Yes, Buitrago del Lozoya, a common starting point for many routes, is accessible by public transport from Madrid. This makes it convenient for cyclists who prefer not to drive to the trailheads.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Guadarrama Natural Park or around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on established trails around Pantano de Puentes Viejas. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations, especially if venturing into protected or restricted areas within the wider Sierra de Guadarrama Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best time for mountain biking around Pantano de Puentes Viejas. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, while winter may bring colder temperatures, though the area is generally rideable year-round.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of the historic town of Buitrago del Lozoya. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Buitrago del Lozoya – Buitrago de Lozoya loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.5 km route specifically designed to showcase these scenic perspectives.

Q: Can I find routes that connect multiple reservoirs in the area?
A: Yes, the region's network of reservoirs allows for routes that link several of them. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes Viejas Dam – El Villar Reservoir and Gorge loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.3 km route that takes you past multiple impressive water bodies.
tips:
Beautiful town in the Sierra de Madrid. The Alczar (castle), the very colorful Puente del Arrabal and the Church of Santa María del Castillo stand out.
It also has the Picasso Museum that brings together an important collection of the ceramic works of the Malaga painter.
Numerous bars, restaurant, supermarkets, and accommodation if necessary

The Puentes Viejas reservoir was built on the Lozoya River and is part of the water supply network known as Canal de Isabel II, which is still in operation after several centuries in use. It is also a panoramic site with very nice views.
